name: The summit of Aitana — Circular from the Font de Partagat
name_original: La cima de Aitana — Circular desde la Font de Partagat
region_intro: Hiking around Benifato offers access to diverse terrain within the Guadalest Valley and the Sierra de Aitana. The region is characterized by mountainous backdrops, natural springs like Font de Partagat, and lush landscapes with olive and almond groves. Geological formations such as La Forata and Passet de la Rabosa are also present. The area includes protected micro-reserves, contributing to its ecological value. Best hiking trails around Benifato The most popular hiking route is The summit of Aitana — Circular from the Font de Partagat, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain towards the summit of Aitana. Another top favourite among local hikers is Blooming Almond Trees – Hiking Among Almond Trees loop from Benifato, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This trail winds through scenic almond groves and offers pleasant valley views. Local hikers also love the Blooming Almond Trees loop from Benifato, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through almond groves, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Benifato is defined by mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Aitana, serene valley paths, and historical ice houses. The network offers options for moderate and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Benifato are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1300 reviews. More than 6700 hikers have used komoot to explore Benifato's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Benifato?
A: Benifato offers a diverse selection of around 15 hiking routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, with 7 moderate and 8 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for most hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes in Benifato?
A: Hiking in Benifato provides a mix of mountainous terrain from the Sierra de Aitana and serene paths through the Guadalest Valley. You'll encounter lush olive and almond groves, dramatic geological formations like La Forata and Passet de la Rabosa, and natural springs such as Font de Partagat.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benifato?
A: Yes, Benifato features several circular routes. For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18937647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blooming Almond Trees – Hiking Among Almond Trees loop from Benifato&lt;/a&gt;. If you're seeking a challenge, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870490269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The summit of Aitana — Circular from the Font de Partagat&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking around Benifato?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore distinctive geological formations like La Forata and Passet de la Rabosa, and visit the beautiful natural springs of Font de Partagat and Font dels Xorrets. The Sierra de Aitana also hosts protected plant and wildlife micro-reserves.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The landscape around Benifato is dotted with ancient 17th-century ice houses, known as 'clots,' such as Clot dels Teixos. You can also find the ruins of Alfofra Castle, which offers stunning views and adds a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Benifato?
A: The trails in Benifato are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 1300 reviews. More than 6700 hikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the stunning mountain backdrops and the peaceful valley paths.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Benifato?
A: While Benifato's trails lean towards moderate and difficult, an easy circular walk starts directly from Benifato village. This route meanders through olive and almond groves and reaches Font de Partagat, offering a pleasant experience without significant challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benifato?
A: Benifato offers enjoyable hiking throughout much of the year. The spring months are particularly beautiful when the almond trees are in bloom. The natural springs and lush landscapes are vibrant, and the weather is generally pleasant for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any natural springs or water features along the routes?
A: Yes, natural springs are a notable feature of the area. Font de Partagat is a very attractive space, known for its pure, crystalline water, and Font dels Xorrets also contributes to the serene atmosphere. These springs are often highlights on local routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Benifato?
A: Parking is generally available near the starting points of many trails, especially in and around Benifato village and at popular access points like Font de Partagat. It's advisable to check specific route details for precise parking information.

Q: What are some nearby attractions I can visit after a hike?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there options for more challenging hikes in Benifato?
A: Benifato is an excellent destination for experienced hikers, with 8 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870490269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The summit of Aitana — Circular from the Font de Partagat&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding ascent to the highest point in the region, offering significant elevation gain and rewarding views.
tips: At 1,559 meters high, the Sierra de Aitana takes the title of the highest mountain in Alicante. The summit itself is located in military territory and cannot be accessed, but you can get very close along a road in good condition. The views from the top are impressive and getting there certainly requires physical effort, but the reward is incredible.

From its 1,255 meters you can see amazing views in all directions. Its top is part of the Sierra de Aitana chain and from here you will undoubtedly have the best panoramic views of Alicante in its entirety.

It is the ruins of an old refrigerator used in the past to collect snow and then be able to make ice from it. It is known that it used to have a roof, although today only the remains of the walls and the well itself remain.
